rawText:
For a president who has spent decades treating oil as the ultimate source of national wealth and geopolitical leverage -- speaking wistfully in the 1980s of seizing Iranian oilfields and more recently pursuing an agenda of "energy dominance" through expanded drilling on federal lands -- Donald Trump now finds himself confronting an uncomfortable truth: obsession with oil does not confer control of it.

The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which roughly a fifth of the world's crude normally passes, has exposed the fragility of American energy power, because even though the shale revolution transformed the United States into a net petroleum exporter for the first time since the 1940s, the country still imports petroleum equivalent to 42% of domestic consumption and lacks the refining infrastructure to process all of its own light, sweet crude.

Trump's arsenal of policy responses -- the largest-ever coordinated release of strategic oil reserves by the International Energy Agency, a $20 billion reinsurance facility for ships in the Gulf, and diplomatic arm-twisting to assemble naval escorts -- has so far failed to impress oil markets, where Brent crude has surged past $110 a barrel and traders remain sceptical that the war will end on the president's promised timetable.

The option of banning oil exports, which insiders say has not yet been placed on the table but which rising petrol prices and approaching midterm elections could make irresistible, would trap four million barrels per day of American crude -- nine percent of global seaborne flows -- while creating severe distortions within domestic markets, where the east coast depends on European gasoline imports and the Pacific coast, disconnected from the national pipeline network, sources fuel from Asia and the Middle East.

Even a combined ban on crude and refined product exports, though it might momentarily protect refiner margins, would strangle the world economy in ways that would inevitably boomerang back to America, destroying the country's hard-won reputation as a reliable supplier and deterring the very investment in oil-and-gas infrastructure that "energy dominance" supposedly demands.

American shale producers, whom Trump cannot compel to drill and who now prioritise returning cash to shareholders rather than expanding output, could add at most 300,000 barrels per day over six to twelve months -- a pittance against the roughly fifteen million barrels per day of oil and products stranded behind Hormuz -- and the vaunted new leases on federal lands remain options for future drilling that industry insiders consider unattractive.

The administration's increasingly desperate search for alternatives, from relaxing secondary sanctions on Russian crude to the reported contemplation of occupying Iran's Kharg Island with ground troops, reveals a leader caught between his mythologised vision of American petroleum supremacy and the stubbornly physical realities of global energy logistics that no presidential decree can override.

Although some Republicans have argued that the energy shock is good for America now that the country is a net fuel exporter, this framing disguises a profound redistribution of wealth within American society -- from coastal consumers to the oil patch, from airlines to energy majors, and most consequentially from the poorest fifth of households, who spend nearly twice as much of their income on petrol and electricity, to the share-owning class that captures windfall profits.

With average petrol prices approaching four dollars a gallon and consumer sentiment already at dismal levels before the war began, the political calculus grows ever more perilous for Republicans, since research consistently shows that pre-election prices at the pump correlate strongly with the governing party's performance at the ballot box -- a dynamic that could hand congressional Democrats and oil companies an improbable shared victory.

The deeper irony of Trump's predicament is that a president who built his energy identity on the promise of cheap, abundant American oil has instead demonstrated, through a war of choice, that in an interconnected global market where fifteen percent of supply can vanish behind a single chokepoint, no volume of domestic production can insulate a nation from the consequences of strategic miscalculation.

overview.title: Trump, Oil, and the Limits of American Energy Power

sentences.original: For a president who has spent decades treating oil as the ultimate source of national wealth and geopolitical leverage -- speaking wistfully in the 1980s of seizing Iranian oilfields and more recently pursuing an agenda of "energy dominance" through expanded drilling on federal lands -- Donald Trump now finds himself confronting an uncomfortable truth: obsession with oil does not confer control of it.
sentences.translation: 석유를 국가 부와 지정학적 영향력의 궁극적 원천으로 취급하며 수십 년을 보내온 대통령, 즉 1980년대에는 이란 유전 점령을 동경 어린 어조로 말하고 최근에는 연방 토지에서의 시추 확대를 통해 '에너지 지배'라는 의제를 추구해 온 도널드 트럼프는 이제 불편한 진실에 직면하고 있다. 석유에 대한 집착이 석유에 대한 통제력을 부여하지는 않는다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: For a president who has spent decades treating oil as ...
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절 (배경 설명)

grammarElements.text: Donald Trump now finds himself confronting an uncomfortable truth
grammarElements.label: 주절 (find + 재귀대명사 + 분사)

grammarElements.text: speaking wistfully ... and more recently pursuing ...
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 분사구문

grammarElements.text: obsession with oil does not confer control of it
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 진술 (핵심 주장)

vocabulary.example: Oil has long served as a source of geopolitical leverage for producing nations.
A strong military does not automatically confer diplomatic influence.

expressions.usage: 본인의 의지와 무관하게 어떤 상황에 맞닥뜨리게 된 것을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: For [someone] who has [long history], [that person] now finds [themselves] confronting [inconvenient reality]
patterns.explanation: 과거의 신념이나 행동과 현재 직면한 현실의 괴리를 드러내는 아이러니 패턴
examples: For a CEO who has always championed growth at all costs, she now finds herself confronting the limits of expansion.
For a party that promised fiscal discipline, it now finds itself confronting record deficits.

drillQuestions.question: Obsession with oil does not ______ control of it.

sentences.notes: 콜론 뒤의 짧고 강렬한 문장이 기사 전체의 핵심 논제를 압축한다. wistfully라는 부사가 트럼프의 석유에 대한 오랜 동경을 효과적으로 전달한다.

koreanAlignment.en: For a president who has spent decades treating oil as the ultimate source
koreanAlignment.ko: 석유를 궁극적 원천으로 취급하며 수십 년을 보내온 대통령

koreanAlignment.en: geopolitical leverage
koreanAlignment.ko: 지정학적 영향력

koreanAlignment.en: Donald Trump now finds himself confronting an uncomfortable truth
koreanAlignment.ko: 트럼프는 이제 불편한 진실에 직면하고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: obsession with oil does not confer control of it
koreanAlignment.ko: 석유에 대한 집착이 석유에 대한 통제력을 부여하지는 않는다

pronunciationNotes: wistfully: /ˈwɪstfəli/ — wist에 강세, f와 l이 빠르게 연결됨

sentences.original: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which roughly a fifth of the world's crude normally passes, has exposed the fragility of American energy power, because even though the shale revolution transformed the United States into a net petroleum exporter for the first time since the 1940s, the country still imports petroleum equivalent to 42% of domestic consumption and lacks the refining infrastructure to process all of its own light, sweet crude.
sentences.translation: 세계 원유의 약 5분의 1이 정상적으로 통과하는 호르무즈 해협의 폐쇄는 미국 에너지 패권의 취약성을 노출시켰는데, 셰일 혁명이 미국을 1940년대 이후 처음으로 순석유 수출국으로 전환시켰음에도 불구하고 여전히 국내 소비량의 42%에 해당하는 석유를 수입하고 있으며 자국의 경질 원유를 모두 처리할 정제 인프라가 부족하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: through which roughly a fifth of the world's crude normally passes
grammarElements.label: 전치사 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: has exposed the fragility of American energy power
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 동사 + 목적어

grammarElements.text: because even though ... the country still imports ...
grammarElements.label: because + even though 복합 원인절

vocabulary.example: The pandemic exposed the fragility of global supply chains.

expressions.usage: 수출량이 수입량을 초과하는 국가를 가리킬 때 사용하는 경제 용어

patterns.pattern: [Event] has exposed the fragility of [perceived strength], because even though [achievement], [entity] still [vulnerability]
patterns.explanation: 어떤 사건이 겉보기 강점의 이면에 숨은 취약성을 노출시키는 패턴
examples: The cyberattack has exposed the fragility of the banking system, because even though regulators modernised oversight, banks still depend on legacy software.
The drought has exposed the fragility of California's water system, because even though the state built reservoirs, it still depends on diminishing snowpack.

drillQuestions.question: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has exposed the ______ of American energy power.

sentences.notes: even though절이 셰일 혁명의 성과를 인정하면서도 still이 지속되는 취약성을 강조한다. light, sweet crude는 유황 함량이 낮은 경질 원유를 가리키는 업계 용어이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz
koreanAlignment.ko: 호르무즈 해협의 폐쇄

koreanAlignment.en: has exposed the fragility of American energy power
koreanAlignment.ko: 미국 에너지 패권의 취약성을 노출시켰다

koreanAlignment.en: even though the shale revolution transformed the United States into a net petroleum exporter
koreanAlignment.ko: 셰일 혁명이 미국을 순석유 수출국으로 전환시켰음에도

koreanAlignment.en: lacks the refining infrastructure
koreanAlignment.ko: 정제 인프라가 부족하다

sentences.original: Trump's arsenal of policy responses -- the largest-ever coordinated release of strategic oil reserves by the International Energy Agency, a $20 billion reinsurance facility for ships in the Gulf, and diplomatic arm-twisting to assemble naval escorts -- has so far failed to impress oil markets, where Brent crude has surged past $110 a barrel and traders remain sceptical that the war will end on the president's promised timetable.
sentences.translation: 국제에너지기구(IEA)에 의한 역대 최대 규모의 전략 석유 비축유 협조 방출, 걸프만 선박을 위한 200억 달러 규모의 재보험 기구, 그리고 해군 호위대를 편성하기 위한 외교적 압박이라는 트럼프의 정책 대응 무기고는 지금까지 유가 시장에 인상적인 영향을 주지 못했으며, 브렌트유는 배럴당 110달러를 돌파했고 트레이더들은 전쟁이 대통령이 약속한 일정대로 끝날 것이라는 데 여전히 회의적이다.

grammarElements.text: Trump's arsenal of policy responses
grammarElements.label: 주어 (은유적 표현)

grammarElements.text: the largest-ever coordinated release ... a $20 billion reinsurance facility ... and diplomatic arm-twisting
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 삼중 동격

grammarElements.text: has so far failed to impress oil markets
grammarElements.label: 현재완료 + fail to

grammarElements.text: where Brent crude has surged past $110
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (장소적)

vocabulary.example: The central bank deployed its full arsenal of monetary tools.

expressions.usage: 설득이 아닌 강압적 방법으로 상대를 움직이게 할 때 사용하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Entity]'s arsenal of [responses] has so far failed to [achieve goal]
patterns.explanation: 총동원한 수단이 아직까지 목표를 달성하지 못했음을 전하는 패턴
examples: The WHO's arsenal of public health measures has so far failed to contain the outbreak.
The government's arsenal of economic stimuli has so far failed to revive consumer spending.

drillQuestions.question: Trump's arsenal of policy responses has so far ______ to impress oil markets.

sentences.notes: arsenal이라는 군사적 은유가 정책 대응을 전투로 프레이밍한다. 세 가지 대응을 삼중 동격으로 나열한 뒤 has failed로 모두 무력화시키는 구조가 효과적이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Trump's arsenal of policy responses
koreanAlignment.ko: 트럼프의 정책 대응 무기고

koreanAlignment.en: the largest-ever coordinated release of strategic oil reserves
koreanAlignment.ko: 역대 최대 규모의 전략 석유 비축유 협조 방출

koreanAlignment.en: has so far failed to impress oil markets
koreanAlignment.ko: 지금까지 유가 시장에 인상적인 영향을 주지 못했다

koreanAlignment.en: traders remain sceptical
koreanAlignment.ko: 트레이더들은 여전히 회의적이다

sentences.original: The option of banning oil exports, which insiders say has not yet been placed on the table but which rising petrol prices and approaching midterm elections could make irresistible, would trap four million barrels per day of American crude -- nine percent of global seaborne flows -- while creating severe distortions within domestic markets, where the east coast depends on European gasoline imports and the Pacific coast, disconnected from the national pipeline network, sources fuel from Asia and the Middle East.
sentences.translation: 내부 관계자들이 아직 테이블 위에 올라오지 않았다고 말하지만 유가 상승과 다가오는 중간선거가 거부할 수 없게 만들 수 있는 석유 수출 금지라는 선택지는, 전 세계 해상 물동량의 9%인 하루 400만 배럴의 미국 원유를 가두게 되는 한편, 동부 해안이 유럽산 휘발유 수입에 의존하고 국가 파이프라인 네트워크에서 단절된 태평양 연안은 아시아와 중동에서 연료를 조달하는 국내 시장에 심각한 왜곡을 초래할 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The option of banning oil exports
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: which insiders say has not yet been placed on the table
grammarElements.label: 관계절 1 (현재 상태)

grammarElements.text: but which rising petrol prices ... could make irresistible
grammarElements.label: 관계절 2 (잠재적 변화)

grammarElements.text: would trap four million barrels per day
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사 (가정법)

grammarElements.text: while creating severe distortions within domestic markets
grammarElements.label: 동시 상황 분사구문

vocabulary.meaning: 거부할 수 없는, 저항할 수 없는
vocabulary.example: Political pressure could make an export ban irresistible despite its economic costs.

expressions.usage: 정책이나 제안이 공식적인 논의 단계에 진입했을 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The option of [drastic measure], which [hasn't happened yet] but which [pressures] could make irresistible, would [consequence A] while [consequence B]
patterns.explanation: 아직 실행되지 않은 극단적 조치의 잠재적 결과를 이중으로 제시하는 분석 패턴
examples: The option of capital controls, which officials deny considering but which a falling currency could make irresistible, would freeze foreign investment while undermining market confidence.
The option of nationalisation, which the party has not endorsed but which public anger could make irresistible, would scare investors while empowering bureaucrats.

drillQuestions.question: Rising petrol prices and approaching midterm elections could make the option of banning oil exports ______.

sentences.notes: 두 개의 which절이 현재(아직 검토되지 않음)와 미래(거부할 수 없게 될 수 있음)를 대비시킨다. 동부와 서부 해안의 구체적인 에너지 의존 구조가 수출 금지의 복잡성을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The option of banning oil exports
koreanAlignment.ko: 석유 수출 금지라는 선택지

koreanAlignment.en: has not yet been placed on the table
koreanAlignment.ko: 아직 테이블 위에 올라오지 않았다

koreanAlignment.en: could make irresistible
koreanAlignment.ko: 거부할 수 없게 만들 수 있다

koreanAlignment.en: while creating severe distortions within domestic markets
koreanAlignment.ko: 국내 시장에 심각한 왜곡을 초래하는 한편

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: scenario analysis (가정적 시나리오의 연쇄 결과 분석)

sentences.original: Even a combined ban on crude and refined product exports, though it might momentarily protect refiner margins, would strangle the world economy in ways that would inevitably boomerang back to America, destroying the country's hard-won reputation as a reliable supplier and deterring the very investment in oil-and-gas infrastructure that "energy dominance" supposedly demands.
sentences.translation: 원유와 정제 제품 수출을 결합한 금지 조치조차도, 비록 일시적으로 정유사의 마진을 보호할 수 있겠지만, 불가피하게 미국에 부메랑으로 되돌아올 방식으로 세계 경제를 질식시키며, 신뢰할 수 있는 공급국이라는 힘겹게 얻은 평판을 파괴하고 '에너지 지배'가 요구한다고 하는 바로 그 석유·가스 인프라 투자를 억제할 것이다.

grammarElements.text: Even a combined ban on crude and refined product exports
grammarElements.label: 주어 (Even 강조)

grammarElements.text: though it might momentarily protect refiner margins
grammarElements.label: 양보절

grammarElements.text: would strangle the world economy
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사 (가정법)

grammarElements.text: destroying ... and deterring ...
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (결과)

vocabulary.example: Sanctions designed to hurt rivals often boomerang back to the imposing country.

expressions.usage: 오랜 노력 끝에 얻은 성과나 평판을 강조할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Even [extreme measure], though it might [short-term benefit], would [severe consequence] in ways that would inevitably boomerang back
patterns.explanation: 극단적 조치의 단기 이점과 불가피한 역효과를 대비시키는 패턴
examples: Even a full trade embargo, though it might temporarily hurt the target, would disrupt supply chains in ways that would inevitably boomerang back to allied economies.
Even a blanket immigration ban, though it might reduce short-term competition, would starve industries in ways that would inevitably boomerang back to growth.

drillQuestions.question: A combined ban would strangle the world economy in ways that would inevitably ______ back to America.

sentences.notes: strangle이라는 동사의 강렬함이 수출 금지의 파괴력을 전달한다. the very investment that energy dominance supposedly demands에서 supposedly가 에너지 지배 정책의 자기모순을 꼬집는다.

koreanAlignment.en: Even a combined ban on crude and refined product exports
koreanAlignment.ko: 원유와 정제 제품 수출을 결합한 금지 조치조차도

koreanAlignment.en: would strangle the world economy
koreanAlignment.ko: 세계 경제를 질식시킬 것이다

koreanAlignment.en: inevitably boomerang back to America
koreanAlignment.ko: 불가피하게 미국에 부메랑으로 되돌아오다

koreanAlignment.en: the country's hard-won reputation as a reliable supplier
koreanAlignment.ko: 신뢰할 수 있는 공급국이라는 힘겹게 얻은 평판

pronunciationNotes: boomerang: /ˈbuːməræŋ/ — boo에 강세, 호주 원주민 언어에서 유래
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: self-defeating irony (에너지 지배 정책이 에너지 투자를 억제)

sentences.original: American shale producers, whom Trump cannot compel to drill and who now prioritise returning cash to shareholders rather than expanding output, could add at most 300,000 barrels per day over six to twelve months -- a pittance against the roughly fifteen million barrels per day of oil and products stranded behind Hormuz -- and the vaunted new leases on federal lands remain options for future drilling that industry insiders consider unattractive.
sentences.translation: 트럼프가 시추를 강제할 수 없으며 현재는 생산량 확대보다 주주에게 현금을 돌려주는 것을 우선시하는 미국 셰일 생산업체들은 6개월에서 12개월에 걸쳐 최대 하루 30만 배럴을 추가할 수 있을 뿐인데, 이는 호르무즈 해협 뒤에 묶인 약 1,500만 배럴의 석유 및 석유 제품에 비하면 하찮은 양이며, 자랑스럽게 내세운 연방 토지의 새 임대권도 업계 관계자들이 매력적이지 않다고 보는 미래 시추 옵션에 불과하다.

grammarElements.text: American shale producers
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: whom Trump cannot compel to drill and who now prioritise returning cash
grammarElements.label: 이중 관계절

grammarElements.text: could add at most 300,000 barrels per day
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사 (가능성 한정)

grammarElements.text: a pittance against ... fifteen million barrels
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격 (대비)

vocabulary.example: The emergency supply increase is a pittance compared to global demand.
The vaunted defence system proved ineffective under real combat conditions.

expressions.usage: 가능한 최대치를 제시하되 그것조차 부족함을 암시할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Producers], whom [authority] cannot compel to [action], could add at most [small amount] -- a pittance against [large need]
patterns.explanation: 공급자를 통제할 수 없으며 그들의 최대 기여분도 필요량에 비해 미미함을 보여주는 패턴
examples: Private hospitals, whom the government cannot compel to accept public patients, could add at most 5,000 beds -- a pittance against the 200,000 needed.
Renewable firms, whom regulators cannot compel to accelerate timelines, could add at most 2 gigawatts -- a pittance against the 30 gigawatts required.

drillQuestions.question: 300,000 barrels per day is a ______ against the fifteen million barrels stranded behind Hormuz.

sentences.notes: 30만 대 1,500만이라는 극적인 수치 대비가 미국 내 생산 확대의 한계를 직관적으로 보여준다. vaunted라는 단어가 연방 토지 임대 정책에 대한 필자의 회의적 시선을 드러낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: American shale producers, whom Trump cannot compel to drill
koreanAlignment.ko: 트럼프가 시추를 강제할 수 없는 미국 셰일 생산업체

koreanAlignment.en: could add at most 300,000 barrels per day
koreanAlignment.ko: 최대 하루 30만 배럴을 추가할 수 있을 뿐이다

koreanAlignment.en: a pittance against the roughly fifteen million barrels
koreanAlignment.ko: 약 1,500만 배럴에 비하면 하찮은 양

koreanAlignment.en: the vaunted new leases on federal lands
koreanAlignment.ko: 자랑스럽게 내세운 연방 토지의 새 임대권

sentences.original: The administration's increasingly desperate search for alternatives, from relaxing secondary sanctions on Russian crude to the reported contemplation of occupying Iran's Kharg Island with ground troops, reveals a leader caught between his mythologised vision of American petroleum supremacy and the stubbornly physical realities of global energy logistics that no presidential decree can override.
sentences.translation: 러시아산 원유에 대한 이차 제재 완화에서부터 지상군으로 이란의 하르그 섬을 점령하는 것에 대한 보도된 검토에 이르기까지, 행정부의 점점 더 절박한 대안 모색은 미국 석유 패권에 대한 자신의 신화화된 비전과 어떤 대통령 행정명령으로도 무시할 수 없는 완고하게 물리적인 세계 에너지 물류의 현실 사이에 끼인 지도자를 드러낸다.

grammarElements.text: The administration's increasingly desperate search for alternatives
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: from relaxing secondary sanctions ... to the reported contemplation of occupying ...
grammarElements.label: from A to B 범위 구문

grammarElements.text: reveals a leader caught between
grammarElements.label: 본동사 + caught between 구문

grammarElements.text: the stubbornly physical realities ... that no presidential decree can override
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 보어

vocabulary.example: The contemplation of military options signals growing desperation.

expressions.usage: 두 가지 상충하는 현실이나 요구 사이에서 선택이 불가능한 상황을 묘사

patterns.pattern: [Entity]'s increasingly desperate search for [solutions], from [measure A] to [measure B], reveals [fundamental dilemma]
patterns.explanation: 점점 극단적이 되는 대안 모색이 근본적 딜레마를 노출시키는 패턴
examples: The company's increasingly desperate search for revenue, from selling assets to exploring mergers, reveals a business model that no longer works.
The party's increasingly desperate search for voters, from populist promises to celebrity endorsements, reveals a platform that has lost touch with reality.

drillQuestions.question: The administration's increasingly desperate search for alternatives reveals a leader caught between his ______ vision and stubbornly physical realities.

sentences.notes: mythologised와 stubbornly physical의 대비가 트럼프의 석유 비전이 현실과 동떨어져 있음을 효과적으로 전달한다. no presidential decree can override가 대통령 권력의 한계를 단정적으로 명시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The administration's increasingly desperate search for alternatives
koreanAlignment.ko: 행정부의 점점 더 절박한 대안 모색

koreanAlignment.en: from relaxing secondary sanctions on Russian crude
koreanAlignment.ko: 러시아산 원유에 대한 이차 제재 완화에서부터

koreanAlignment.en: to the reported contemplation of occupying Iran's Kharg Island
koreanAlignment.ko: 이란의 하르그 섬 점령에 대한 보도된 검토에 이르기까지

koreanAlignment.en: no presidential decree can override
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 대통령 행정명령으로도 무시할 수 없다

sentences.original: Although some Republicans have argued that the energy shock is good for America now that the country is a net fuel exporter, this framing disguises a profound redistribution of wealth within American society -- from coastal consumers to the oil patch, from airlines to energy majors, and most consequentially from the poorest fifth of households, who spend nearly twice as much of their income on petrol and electricity, to the share-owning class that captures windfall profits.
sentences.translation: 일부 공화당 의원들은 미국이 순연료 수출국인 지금 에너지 충격이 미국에 이롭다고 주장하지만, 이 프레이밍은 미국 사회 내부의 심대한 부의 재분배를 감추고 있다. 해안 지역 소비자에서 산유 지역으로, 항공사에서 주요 에너지 기업으로, 그리고 가장 중요하게는 소득의 거의 두 배를 휘발유와 전기에 쓰는 최빈곤 5분위 가구에서 횡재 이익을 거머쥐는 주식 보유 계층으로의 재분배이다.

grammarElements.text: Although some Republicans have argued that ...
grammarElements.label: 양보절

grammarElements.text: this framing disguises a profound redistribution of wealth
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: from ... to ..., from ... to ..., and ... from ... to ...
grammarElements.label: 삼중 from-to 대비 구조

grammarElements.text: who spend nearly twice as much of their income
grammarElements.label: 관계절

vocabulary.example: High energy prices drive a silent redistribution of wealth from poor to rich.
Oil companies are enjoying windfall profits while consumers struggle with high prices.

expressions.usage: 표면적 주장 뒤에 숨은 진실을 드러낼 때 사용

patterns.pattern: Although [optimistic argument], this framing disguises [hidden cost] -- from [loser A] to [winner A], from [loser B] to [winner B]
patterns.explanation: 낙관적 주장의 이면에 숨겨진 승자와 패자를 구체적으로 밝히는 반박 패턴
examples: Although boosters claim globalisation lifts all boats, this framing disguises a transfer of wealth from workers to shareholders.
Although defenders argue that automation creates more jobs than it destroys, this framing disguises a shift from stable careers to gig work.

drillQuestions.question: This framing ______ a profound redistribution of wealth within American society.

sentences.notes: 삼중 from-to 구조가 에너지 충격의 재분배 효과를 단계적으로 보여주며, most consequentially로 가장 심각한 불평등을 마지막에 배치해 수사적 효과를 극대화한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Although some Republicans have argued that the energy shock is good for America
koreanAlignment.ko: 일부 공화당 의원들은 에너지 충격이 미국에 이롭다고 주장하지만

koreanAlignment.en: this framing disguises a profound redistribution of wealth
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 프레이밍은 심대한 부의 재분배를 감추고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: from the poorest fifth of households
koreanAlignment.ko: 최빈곤 5분위 가구에서

koreanAlignment.en: the share-owning class that captures windfall profits
koreanAlignment.ko: 횡재 이익을 거머쥐는 주식 보유 계층으로

sentences.original: With average petrol prices approaching four dollars a gallon and consumer sentiment already at dismal levels before the war began, the political calculus grows ever more perilous for Republicans, since research consistently shows that pre-election prices at the pump correlate strongly with the governing party's performance at the ballot box -- a dynamic that could hand congressional Democrats and oil companies an improbable shared victory.
sentences.translation: 평균 휘발유 가격이 갤런당 4달러에 육박하고 소비자 심리가 전쟁 시작 전부터 이미 참담한 수준이었던 상황에서, 공화당에게 정치적 계산은 갈수록 위태로워지고 있는데, 연구가 일관되게 보여주듯 선거 전 주유소 가격은 집권당의 투표소 성적과 강하게 상관관계를 가지며, 이 역학이 의회 민주당과 석유 회사들에게 있을 법하지 않은 공동 승리를 안겨줄 수도 있기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: With average petrol prices approaching four dollars a gallon
grammarElements.label: with 독립분사구문 (배경)

grammarElements.text: the political calculus grows ever more perilous for Republicans
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: since research consistently shows that ...
grammarElements.label: since 이유절

grammarElements.text: a dynamic that could hand ... an improbable shared victory
grammarElements.label: 대시 삽입 동격 (결론)

vocabulary.example: The political calculus grows perilous when voters feel the squeeze at the pump.

expressions.usage: 소비자가 직접 체감하는 연료 가격을 가리킬 때 사용하는 일상적 표현

patterns.pattern: With [negative indicator] and [another negative indicator], the political calculus grows ever more perilous for [party], since [evidence]
patterns.explanation: 부정적 지표들이 정치적 위험을 가중시키는 상황을 분석하는 패턴
examples: With unemployment rising and housing costs surging, the political calculus grows ever more perilous for the incumbent, since voters consistently blame the party in power.
With trust in institutions falling and protest movements growing, the political calculus grows ever more perilous for the ruling coalition.

drillQuestions.question: The political calculus grows ever more ______ for Republicans.

sentences.notes: prices at the pump과 performance at the ballot box라는 두 개의 at 전치사구가 주유소와 투표소를 직접 연결시킨다. improbable shared victory라는 표현이 민주당과 석유 회사라는 기이한 조합의 수혜를 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: With average petrol prices approaching four dollars a gallon
koreanAlignment.ko: 평균 휘발유 가격이 갤런당 4달러에 육박하는 가운데

koreanAlignment.en: the political calculus grows ever more perilous for Republicans
koreanAlignment.ko: 공화당에게 정치적 계산은 갈수록 위태로워지고 있다

koreanAlignment.en: pre-election prices at the pump correlate strongly with the governing party's performance at the ballot box
koreanAlignment.ko: 선거 전 주유소 가격은 집권당의 투표소 성적과 강하게 상관관계를 가진다

koreanAlignment.en: an improbable shared victory
koreanAlignment.ko: 있을 법하지 않은 공동 승리

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: cause-and-effect chain (주유소 가격에서 선거 결과까지의 인과)

sentences.original: The deeper irony of Trump's predicament is that a president who built his energy identity on the promise of cheap, abundant American oil has instead demonstrated, through a war of choice, that in an interconnected global market where fifteen percent of supply can vanish behind a single chokepoint, no volume of domestic production can insulate a nation from the consequences of strategic miscalculation.
sentences.translation: 트럼프의 곤경에 담긴 더 깊은 아이러니는, 값싸고 풍부한 미국산 석유의 약속 위에 에너지 정체성을 구축한 대통령이 오히려 선택에 의한 전쟁을 통해, 공급량의 15%가 단 하나의 병목 지점 뒤로 사라질 수 있는 상호 연결된 글로벌 시장에서는 아무리 많은 국내 생산량도 전략적 오판의 결과로부터 한 나라를 보호할 수 없다는 것을 입증했다는 점이다.

grammarElements.text: The deeper irony of Trump's predicament
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is that
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사 + that절

grammarElements.text: a president who built his energy identity on the promise of ...
grammarElements.label: that절 내 주어 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: has instead demonstrated
grammarElements.label: that절 내 동사 (instead 대비)

grammarElements.text: no volume of domestic production can insulate a nation from ...
grammarElements.label: 내포 that절 (핵심 결론)

vocabulary.example: No amount of domestic reserves can insulate a country from global price shocks.
The Strait of Hormuz remains the world's most critical energy chokepoint.

expressions.meaning: 선택에 의한 전쟁, 불가피하지 않은 전쟁
expressions.usage: 자위가 아닌 정치적 결정에 의해 시작된 전쟁을 가리킬 때 사용하며, 이라크 전쟁 논쟁에서 유래

patterns.pattern: The deeper irony is that [someone] who [promised X] has instead demonstrated that [opposite of X]
patterns.explanation: 약속과 결과의 정반대를 보여주는 최종적 아이러니 진술 패턴
examples: The deeper irony is that a leader who promised peace has instead demonstrated that unilateral action breeds more conflict.
The deeper irony is that a company that promised privacy has instead demonstrated that user data is its most valuable product.

drillQuestions.question: No volume of domestic production can ______ a nation from the consequences of strategic miscalculation.

sentences.notes: 기사의 마지막 문장으로서 전체 논지를 요약한다. has instead demonstrated가 약속과 결과의 정반대를 날카롭게 대비시킨다. war of choice는 이라크 전쟁 논쟁에서 유래한 정치적 함의가 큰 표현이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The deeper irony of Trump's predicament
koreanAlignment.ko: 트럼프의 곤경에 담긴 더 깊은 아이러니

koreanAlignment.en: a president who built his energy identity on the promise of cheap, abundant American oil
koreanAlignment.ko: 값싸고 풍부한 미국산 석유의 약속 위에 에너지 정체성을 구축한 대통령

koreanAlignment.en: has instead demonstrated
koreanAlignment.ko: 오히려 입증했다

koreanAlignment.en: no volume of domestic production can insulate a nation
koreanAlignment.ko: 아무리 많은 국내 생산량도 한 나라를 보호할 수 없다

koreanAlignment.en: the consequences of strategic miscalculation
koreanAlignment.ko: 전략적 오판의 결과

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: grand irony (기사 전체를 요약하는 최종적 아이러니)
